query: maternal age association with preterm labor in low resource settings

retrieved_docs.title: Small for Gestational Age (Archived) -- Epidemiology
retrieved_docs.content: The prevalence of FGR SGA varies across different populations. [11] [12] [13] The prevalent rate for FGR SGA is higher in resource-poor countries, with the highest burden in South Asia, where up to 34% of infants were born FGR SGA. [11] [14] In comparison, approximately 11% of infants in rich and developed countries were born FGR SGA. [4] [5] Children born FGR SGA have a higher risk of mortality and morbidity during the neonatal period and beyond. An estimated 22% of neonatal deaths are attributed to being born FGR SGA, with the highest number reported in South Asia. [11] The incidence rates of FGR SGA in preterm infants vary depending on whether data collection was based on birth weight or gestational age. In a cohort of 4438 infants with birthweight from 500 to 1500g, 22% are reported as FGR SGA, according to the National Institute of Child Health and Human Development (NICHD) Neonatal Research Network database. [15] In other cohorts of 20,000 infants with very low birth of < 1500g and gestational age between 25 to 30 weeks, an estimated 9% of these infants were born SGA.

retrieved_docs.title: Small for Gestational Age (Archived) -- Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: Small for gestational age (SGA) is defined as a birth weight of less than the 10th percentile for gestational age. [1] SGA infants are categorized into 2 major groups: constitutionally normal infants who are SGA and infants who are SGA because of growth restriction with a birth weight lower than the expected optimal birth weight. The constitutionally normal infants have normal birth weights less than the 10th percentile because of inherent factors such as maternal height, weight, ethnicity, and parity. There is no increased risk of perinatal mortality and morbidity in these infants. [1] SGA and fetal growth restriction (FGR) are interchangeable but not synonymous. Many infants who are SGA have FGR, and many infants with FGR are SGA. However, SGA cannot be used as a marker for FGR because some infants with FGR have a birth weight greater than the 10th percentile. Therefore, in making a distinction between SGA and FGR, it is important to use fetal growth curves customized based on constitutional factors to distinguish normal SGA infants from those with FGR. [2] This topic focused on infants with SGA secondary to FGR (FGR SGA). The prevalence of FGR SGA in high-income countries such as the United States and Australia is approximately 11%; however, in low and middle-income countries, an estimated 32.5 million infants were born FGR SGA, and the majority of these infants, an estimated 53% (16.8 million), were born in South Asia. [3] [4] [5] The causes of FGR SGA are diverse and range from fetal, maternal, uterine/placental to demographic factors. Recent studies have shown that catch-up growth is possible, and normal size can be reached at 9 months in about 80% of newborns with FGR SGA. [6] [7] However, newborns with FGR SGA are at increased risk for other complications such as prematurity, neonatal asphyxia, hypothermia, hypoglycemia, hypocalcemia, polycythemia, sepsis, and death. [8] The purpose of this review is to highlight the current trends in the management of children born with FGR SGA.

retrieved_docs.title: Antepartum Infections -- Epidemiology
retrieved_docs.content: Worldwide, sepsis is a leading cause of maternal morbidity and mortality, as there is frequently a delay in diagnosis and suboptimal medical management. [8] To address this issue, the WHO launched the Global Maternal Sepsis and Neonatal Initiative in collaboration with its partners in 2016. This initiative led to the implementation of the GLOSS (Global Maternal Sepsis Study) in 2017, aiming to reduce the burden of maternal sepsis and related infections. While some progress has been made in alleviating the impact of maternal sepsis and maternal infections (MSMI) across 131 LMICs, the disease burden remains significantly higher in low-income countries compared to lower-middle- and upper-middle-income countries. Factors such as socioeconomic status and access to universal health coverage play a crucial role in shaping the severity of MSMI in these regions. [7]

retrieved_docs.title: Preterm Labor -- Epidemiology
retrieved_docs.content: In patients presenting with signs or symptoms of PTL, only 5.5% deliver within 1 week. [3] Even in patients with a clinical diagnosis of sPTL, less than 10% give birth within 7 days of presentation. [1] [3] The worldwide incidence of PTB is approximately 10%, with 40% to 45% of those births occurring secondary to sPTL with intact membranes. [7] [13] This rate tends to be higher in lower-resource settings and lower in high-resource settings. Regarding racial disparities, results from a large retrospective analysis of patients treated at Kaiser Permanente in California between 2009 and 2020 found statistically significant differences in rates of sPTB by race. [14] In this study, the results showed that the incidence of sPTB in non-Hispanic Black individuals was 5.5% compared to 3.6% in non-Hispanic White individuals ( P < 0.001).

retrieved_docs.title: Small for Gestational Age (Archived) -- Prognosis
retrieved_docs.content: Perinatal morbidity and mortality are increased in infants with FGR SGA compared to those appropriate for gestational age (AGA) both in term and preterm newborns. [4] [43] [44] [45] [46] [47] [48] [49] [50] The increase in perinatal mortality reported in a population-based study showed the highest rate in infants with severe FGR SGA (birth weight < 5 percentile of gestational age.) [51] FGR SGA is a risk factor for death in term infants, as reported in many studies. The death rate has been shown to increase with decreasing birthweight percentiles. [49] [50] In 1 study of preterm live births, the death rates for children born between 24 and 28 weeks gestation age were 62%, 42%, and 30% for those who were SGA (birth weight <10th percentile), mildly FGR SGA (birth weight between 10th and <20th percentile) and AGA (birth weight > or =20th percentile). On the other hand, children born FGR SGA between 29- and 32 weeks gestation had not only higher mortality but also minor cognitive difficulties, inattention-hyperactivity symptoms, and school difficulties compared to AGA infants. [52]

retrieved_docs.title: Preterm Labor -- Continuing Education Activity
retrieved_docs.content: Objectives: Identify risk factors for spontaneous preterm labor and underlying etiologies that can trigger the condition. Apply the evaluation of preterm labor, including when to use fetal fibronectin testing and how to interpret the results. Differentiate the management of preterm labor based on the patient's gestational age. Communicate the role of collaboration among interprofessional team members to improve care coordination, leading to earlier recognition of preterm labor and optimal management and outcomes. Access free multiple choice questions on this topic.

retrieved_docs.title: Retinopathy of Prematurity -- Epidemiology -- Risk Factors Influencing Epidemiology
retrieved_docs.content: Several neonatal and institutional risk factors that influence the epidemiology of ROP are as follows: Gestational age and birth weight: These are the most consistent predictors. Infants born before 28 weeks or weighing <1000 g have the highest risk. Oxygen therapy practices: Inconsistent or excessive oxygen supplementation can significantly increase the incidence, especially in units without oxygen monitoring. Resource setting: In high-resource NICUs, ROP is more often restricted to extremely preterm infants, while in low-resource settings, even infants with birth weights up to 2000 g may develop ROP. NICU infrastructure and staffing: The availability of trained neonatologists, oxygen blenders, and nursing ratios influences outcomes. Screening policies: Countries with national ROP screening guidelines (eg, the United Kingdom,  the United States, Brazil) report lower rates of blindness compared to countries with fragmented or absent screening protocols. [33]

retrieved_docs.title: Clinical Practice Issues in American Indians and Alaska Natives -- Treatment / Management -- Indian Health Services
retrieved_docs.content: When IHS came into being, the disparity in the health of the AIAN community seemed insurmountable. The infant mortality rate (IMR) was three times higher than other races, and life expectancy was nine years lower than the average population. A quarter of all deaths occurred in infancy before attaining one year of age. Maternal deaths associated with childbirth were three times higher than NHW. Infectious diseases like diarrhea, tuberculosis, chlamydia eye infection were rampant. Despite the chronic issues faced by the IHS (listed above), there are some notable achievements of the organization in reducing the disparities in the healthcare of the AIAN population.

retrieved_docs.title: Ballard and Dubowitz Neonatal Assessments for Gestational Age Determination -- Summary / Explanation -- Introduction
retrieved_docs.content: Antenatal ultrasound is a reliable method for determining gestational age in the first trimester. However, it has significant limitations, including reduced accuracy in later pregnancy, high cost, limited accessibility in resource-limited settings, and the need for trained personnel. [4] In contrast, dating from the LMP is cost-effective and simple, yet estimation issues often arise from uncertainties such as irregular menstruation, which can result from nutritional deficiencies or maternal illnesses prevalent in low- and middle-income regions. [5] In developed countries, healthcare providers commonly use first-trimester ultrasonographic dating and dating from the LMP for gestational age estimation.

retrieved_docs.title: Ballard and Dubowitz Neonatal Assessments for Gestational Age Determination -- Summary / Explanation -- Expanded New Ballard Scoring System
retrieved_docs.content: Recent research has focused on improving gestational age assessment for preterm newborns using machine learning techniques and smartphone applications. Patel et al developed the Tablet App for the Simplified Gestational Age Score, a simple and inexpensive tool for estimating gestational age, particularly valuable in resource-limited settings to facilitate early identification of preterm neonates, optimize health resource allocation, and improve preterm birth management. [18] Machine learning models are being applied to metabolomic gestational age dating, which estimates gestational age by analyzing specific metabolic markers in the neonate's blood that reflect physiological processes varying with fetal maturity, offering a potentially accurate assessment method during the first week of life. [19] [20]

retrieved_docs.content: Ethical and resource considerations In low- and middle-income countries, balancing limited laser/anti-VEGF availability against a high disease burden is crucial; regional ROP programs and training can help expand capacity. [168]

retrieved_docs.title: Small for Gestational Age (Archived) -- Deterrence and Patient Education
retrieved_docs.content: Infants born with FGR SGA are at increased risk of short- and long-term complications and death. Conditions such as maternal hypertension, kidney disease, infections, malnutrition, smoking, and substance abuse increase the risk of having an FGR SGA infant. The evaluation of FGR SGA begins during the antenatal period. Pregnancies with FGR SGA fetuses can be detected with fundal height measurement and routine ultrasound exams during antenatal clinic visits. The early detection of fetuses at risk of FGR SGA helps in planning delivery and initial treatment. At birth, infants born with FGR SGA are thin, have loose peeling skin and decreased muscle mass, and may appear shrunken with wrinkled faces. In some, the head is larger than the rest of the body. Treatment of FGR SGA newborns is primarily focused on preventing and managing complications. Special attention is paid to the newborn’s temperature, and blood glucose is monitored and managed appropriately. Newborns needing more extensive care are admitted into the neonatal intensive care unit. In contrast, those with less need for monitoring can be kept in a mother’s room or a normal newborn nursery. The rate of death and disability is higher in infants born with lower gestational age and birth weight percentile.

retrieved_docs.title: Postpartum Cardiomyopathy(Archived) -- Deterrence and Patient Education
retrieved_docs.content: PPCM results in a significant financial strain on healthcare resources due to the increased disability-adjusted life years, especially in low- and middle-income countries. To reduce disparities and mortality, resources may need to be directed to socially vulnerable communities.

retrieved_docs.title: Normal Labor: Physiology, Evaluation, and Management -- Epidemiology
retrieved_docs.content: A large cohort study from London involving over 122,000 nulliparous women with singleton gestations and spontaneous labor found that the median gestational age at delivery was 39 weeks in Black and Asian women and 40 weeks in white European women. [6] This same study found that 8% of Black women, 7% of Asian women, and 5% of European women delivered before 37 weeks estimated gestational age.

retrieved_docs.title: Antepartum Infections -- Epidemiology
retrieved_docs.content: Low- and middle-income countries (LMICs) continue to face challenges with preventing and managing maternal infections, as limited resources and restricted access to healthcare heighten the risk of adverse pregnancy outcomes. According to a systematic analysis by the World Health Organization (WHO), direct obstetric infections remain a significant cause of maternal mortality, responsible for approximately 10.7% of maternal deaths. This mortality rate is notably higher in LMICs compared to high-income countries (HICs), where the rate stands at 4.7%. [7]

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 4 -- Community 2849
retrieved_docs.content: Poor reproductive history and elective termination of pregnancy are clinically associated with preterm labor (مخاض قبل الأوان). This relationship indicates that women who have experienced a suboptimal reproductive history or who have undergone an elective termination of pregnancy may be at increased risk for developing preterm labor. Recognizing these associations can help clinicians identify patients who require closer monitoring and targeted interventions to prevent or manage preterm labor, thereby improving maternal and neonatal outcomes.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 9 -- Level 3 -- Community 2316
retrieved_docs.content: A poor reproductive history, reflecting previous obstetric complications or adverse outcomes, is clinically associated with an increased risk of preterm labor (مخاض قبل الأوان). This connection highlights the importance of detailed reproductive assessments in prenatal care to identify women who may be more likely to experience early delivery and to apply targeted monitoring and preventive strategies.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 13 -- Level 3 -- Community 1721
retrieved_docs.content: Infant preterm low birth weight, early or threatened labor, threatened preterm labor (تهديد بالمخاض المبتسر), short stature (قصر القامة), and neonatal death (وفاة حديث الولادة) are interrelated clinical concepts. Infant preterm low birth weight is clinically associated with short stature, indicating that babies born prematurely with low weight are more likely to develop reduced adult height. Short stature itself is clinically associated with neonatal death, suggesting that children who remain short have an increased risk of dying in the newborn period. Early or threatened labor is used to classify threatened preterm labor, and threatened preterm labor is also clinically associated with short stature. Together, these relationships highlight that low birth weight and threatened preterm labor can contribute to short stature, which in turn is linked to higher neonatal mortality, underscoring the importance of monitoring growth and labor complications in perinatal care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 6 -- Level 3 -- Community 1449
retrieved_docs.content: Preterm low‑birth‑weight infants are clinically associated with قصر القامة (short stature). Short stature itself is clinically associated with وفاة حديث الولادة (neonatal death), indicating that children who remain short may have an increased risk of dying in the newborn period. Additionally, تهديد بالمخاض المبتسر (threatened preterm labor) is clinically associated with قصر القامة, suggesting that pregnancies complicated by early labor threats often result in offspring who develop short stature. Collectively, these relationships highlight short stature as a common clinical link among preterm low birth weight, threatened preterm labor, and adverse neonatal outcomes such as neonatal death, underscoring its relevance for risk assessment and early intervention in perinatal care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 34 -- Level 4 -- Community 2849
retrieved_docs.content: Cesarean section is identified as a specific type of the Arabic term “الولادة القيصرية” (cesarean birth). The condition described as INFANT PRETERM LOW BIRTH WEIGHT is clinically associated with “الولادة القيصرية”, indicating that cesarean deliveries are linked to the occurrence of preterm infants with low birth weight. This connection underscores the need for careful obstetric decision‑making and targeted neonatal care when a cesarean birth is performed.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 10 -- Level 3 -- Community 1610
retrieved_docs.content: INFANT PRETERM LOW BIRTH WEIGHT is clinically associated with الولادة القيصرية (Cesarean delivery) and with قصر القامة (short stature). In addition, تهديد بالمخاض المبتسر (threatened preterm labor) is clinically associated with قصر القامة. These relationships indicate that short stature is linked both to infants born preterm with low birth weight and to the condition of threatened preterm labor, while preterm low‑birth‑weight infants are more likely to be delivered by Cesarean section. Recognizing these interconnections can help clinicians anticipate delivery method needs and monitor growth outcomes in infants at risk for or experiencing preterm birth complications.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 28 -- Level 3 -- Community 1697
retrieved_docs.content: A caesarean section scar of unknown type and a poor reproductive history are clinically associated with مخاض قبل الأوان (preterm labor), indicating that women who have an indeterminate type of previous cesarean scar or who have experienced suboptimal reproductive outcomes are at increased risk for premature labor. Recognizing these associations enables clinicians to identify high‑risk patients, implement closer surveillance, and consider preventive strategies to reduce the likelihood of preterm delivery.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 3 -- Level 4 -- Community 2855
retrieved_docs.content: A poor reproductive history (REPRODUCTIVE HISTORY POOR) is clinically associated with preterm labor (مخاض قبل الأوان), and preterm labor (مخاض قبل الأوان) is clinically associated with pelvic inflammatory disease (مرض التهاب الحوض). This interrelationship indicates that women with an adverse reproductive background may have an elevated risk of experiencing premature labor, and that premature labor may be linked to underlying pelvic inflammatory disease, underscoring the importance of comprehensive pelvic health assessment in patients with a history of poor reproductive outcomes.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 1 -- Level 4 -- Community 2825
retrieved_docs.content: INFANT PRETERM LOW BIRTH WEIGHT is clinically associated with قصر القامة (short stature), indicating that infants born preterm with low birth weight often experience growth restriction that results in reduced adult height. In turn, قصر القامة is clinically associated with وفاة حديث الولادة (neonatal death), suggesting that short stature in this context reflects severe underlying health challenges that increase the risk of death shortly after birth. Recognizing the link between low‑birth‑weight preterm infants, subsequent short stature, and heightened neonatal mortality can guide clinicians in early monitoring and intervention strategies to improve outcomes for this vulnerable population.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 5 -- Level 4 -- Community 2821
retrieved_docs.content: Threat of premature labor (تهديد بالمخاض المبتسر) is clinically associated with short stature (قصر القامة), indicating that individuals who are shorter in height may have an increased risk of experiencing preterm labor. This association highlights the importance of recognizing short stature as a potential clinical indicator when assessing pregnant patients for the likelihood of premature delivery, thereby informing monitoring strategies and preventive care measures.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 18 -- Level 3 -- Community 1741
retrieved_docs.content: Intrauterine pregnancy is closely linked to a range of obstetric events. It is clinically associated with “other pregnancy and delivery including normal,” indicating that a normally progressing intrauterine gestation often leads to standard delivery outcomes. At the same time, intrauterine pregnancy shows clinical associations with cesarean delivery (الولادة القيصرية), endometritis (الْتِهابُ بِطانَةِ الرَّحِمِ), and the procedure described as ربط البوق, suggesting that these conditions frequently occur together in the context of intrauterine gestation. Cesarean delivery (الولادة القيصرية) itself is clinically associated with preterm birth (ولادة قبل الأوان) and with infant outcomes such as preterm low birth weight (INFANT PRETERM LOW BIRTH WEIGHT), highlighting the potential impact of surgical delivery on neonatal maturity and weight. Additionally, cesarean delivery co‑occurs with ربط البوق, reinforcing the connection between this surgical approach and the aforementioned procedure. “Other pregnancy and delivery including normal” co‑occurs with preterm birth (ولادة قبل الأوان) and also has a relationship with cesarean delivery (الولادة القيصرية), indicating that even pregnancies classified as normal can be linked to early delivery and may sometimes require surgical intervention. The condition described as حَمْلٌ بُوقِيّ co‑occurs with ربط البوق, further emphasizing the recurrent association of ربط البوق with various pregnancy‑related states. Overall, the network of relationships demonstrates that intrauterine pregnancy, normal delivery, preterm birth, cesarean delivery, infant preterm low birth weight, endometritis, and the procedure ربط البوق are interwoven in clinical practice, with multiple conditions frequently occurring together or influencing one another, which is essential information for comprehensive obstetric assessment and patient management.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 4 -- Community 2842
retrieved_docs.content: Infant preterm low birth weight is clinically associated with short stature (قصر القامة), and short stature (قصر القامة) is clinically associated with a threat of premature labor (تهديد بالمخاض المبتسر). This interrelationship indicates that infants born preterm with low birth weight are more likely to develop reduced height, and that reduced height itself may signal an increased risk of threatened premature labor. Recognizing these connections can help clinicians monitor growth patterns and anticipate obstetric complications, thereby improving patient care.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 13 -- Level 4 -- Community 2862
retrieved_docs.content: Early or threatened labor is classified as تهديد بالمخاض المبتسر, indicating a condition in which signs of labor appear prematurely and the situation is identified as threatened preterm labor, a designation that guides clinical monitoring and intervention.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 0 -- Level 2 -- Community 1051
retrieved_docs.content: The domain of LABOR AND PARTURITION encompasses the obstetric condition referred to in Arabic as مخاض مهدد (threatened labor). Early or threatened labor is classified as مخاض مهدد, indicating that this specific presentation falls within the broader category of threatened labor under the umbrella of LABOR AND PARTURITION. Understanding that مخاض مهدد is a distinct subset of labor and parturition aids clinicians in recognizing early signs of labor complications and guides appropriate monitoring and management strategies.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 18 -- Level 4 -- Community 2789
retrieved_docs.content: Other pregnancy and delivery including normal is closely linked with ولادة قبل الأوان, as the two conditions frequently co‑occur. In addition, other pregnancy and delivery including normal has a relationship with الولادة القيصرية, indicating that normal pregnancy and delivery contexts are also associated with Cesarean delivery. الولادة القيصرية is clinically associated with ولادة قبل الأوان, showing that Cesarean delivery often occurs in the setting of preterm birth. Furthermore, INFANT PRETERM LOW BIRTH WEIGHT is clinically associated with الولادة القيصرية, highlighting that infants born preterm with low birth weight are commonly delivered by Cesarean section. Together, these interconnections underscore the overlapping nature of normal pregnancy and delivery categories, preterm birth, Cesarean delivery, and low‑birth‑weight infants in clinical practice.

retrieved_docs.title: UMLS -- Run 22 -- Level 4 -- Community 2763
retrieved_docs.content: Short gestation, low birth weight, and fetal growth retardation constitute a severe perinatal condition that is classified as an extremely premature birth of the highest degree with unspecified weight (ولادة بأقصى درجات الابتسار، غير محددة {الوزن}). This classification highlights that the infant is born far before term, exhibits markedly reduced body weight, and shows evidence of impaired intra‑uterine growth, all of which place the newborn at high risk for morbidity and mortality and necessitate intensive neonatal monitoring and intervention.
